chapters.title: Ammu and Her Family
activity_summary: Coloring pictures of family
Identifying family members and their names
Singing songs about family and the alphabet
Drawing personal home and school
Writing one's own name
Practicing letter formation for A, B, C, D

chapters.title: AMMU AND HER FRIENDS AT HER SCHOOL
activity_summary: Coloring pictures of Ammu and friends at school
Observing and describing various school activities
Story comprehension about a pet squirrel in the classroom
Identifying and expressing favorite colors
Naming and coloring clothes
Learning about hygiene practices like washing hands, legs, and face
Singing songs related to hygiene and school activities
Identifying and naming parts of the body
Drawing and discussing personal pets
Practicing letter recognition and writing for M, N, O, P

chapters.title: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES
activity_summary: Colouring pictures of fruits and vegetables
Answering questions about a market visit
Discussing food preferences and healthy eating habits
Singing a song about fruits
Categorizing various food items as fruits or vegetables
Reading a story about vegetables competing for an award

concept_nodes.label: LOGICAL_REASONING
concept_nodes.description: Skills involving identification, classification, pattern recognition, and understanding relationships.

concept_nodes.label: NUMBER_SENSE
concept_nodes.description: Understanding quantities, counting, and number relationships.

concept_nodes.label: VISUAL_PATTERNING
concept_nodes.description: Recognizing, creating, and representing visual forms and spatial arrangements.

topics.name: Introducing Ammu's Family
topics.summary: This topic introduces Ammu, her family members, and their daily life through a narrative and visual context, encouraging students to observe and identify characters.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Introducing Ammu's Family: Students learn to identify Ammu and her family members through a story and visual cues, fostering observational and identification skills.
mastery_signals: Can point out and name Ammu and other family members in the picture
Can describe simple actions depicted in the story
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about who is in the picture and what they are doing

topics.name: Ammu and Bittu the Squirrel
topics.summary: This topic presents a short story about Ammu's interaction with a squirrel named Bittu and her dog, focusing on simple dialogue and narrative comprehension.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Ammu and Bittu the Squirrel: Students engage with a story featuring Ammu, her dog, and a squirrel, practicing dialogue comprehension and narrative sequencing.
mastery_signals: Can retell the main events of the story
Can identify the characters involved in the dialogue
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about character actions and dialogue

topics.name: Identifying Family Members and Names
topics.summary: Students learn to identify and name different family members, including their specific names, through a song and direct questions, reinforcing vocabulary and social roles.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Identifying Family Members and Names: Students learn to recognize and name family roles and specific names through a song and interactive questions, enhancing vocabulary and social understanding.
mastery_signals: Can correctly identify and name Ammu's family members
Can sing the family song accurately
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about who is singing and the names of family members

topics.name: Personal Family Identification
topics.summary: This topic provides activities for students to identify and count their own family members, connecting the learned concepts to their personal lives.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Personal Family Identification: Students engage in activities to identify and count their own family members, applying concepts of family roles and number sense to their personal context.
mastery_signals: Can correctly list and count their own family members
Can differentiate between various family roles
assessment_indicators: Completes the family member identification chart
Accurately counts the number of family members

topics.name: Representing Personal Spaces
topics.summary: This topic encourages students to draw and describe their home and school, fostering personal connection, creative expression, and spatial representation skills.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Representing Personal Spaces: Students draw and describe their home and school, developing spatial representation and personal connection to their environment.
mastery_signals: Can draw a recognizable representation of their home or school
Can verbally describe features of their drawing
assessment_indicators: Produces a drawing of their home or school
Writes a simple sentence about their drawing

topics.name: Name Recognition and Personalization
topics.summary: This topic focuses on recognizing names of characters from the story and personalizing the learning by drawing a self-portrait and writing one's own name.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Name Recognition and Personalization: Students practice recognizing character names and personalize learning by drawing themselves and writing their own name, reinforcing self-identification and literacy.
mastery_signals: Can correctly match names to characters
Can write their own name legibly
assessment_indicators: Identifies names associated with pictures
Draws a self-portrait and writes their name

topics.name: Alphabet Introduction (A-D)
topics.summary: This topic introduces the first four letters of the English alphabet (A, B, C, D) through songs, visual examples of words starting with these letters, and writing practice.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Family — Alphabet Introduction (A-D): Students learn to recognize and write the letters A, B, C, and D through songs, visual aids, and copying exercises, building foundational literacy skills.
common_misconceptions: Students may confuse uppercase and lowercase letters
Students may struggle with correct letter formation
mastery_signals: Can identify uppercase and lowercase forms of A, B, C, D
Can correctly write the letters A, B, C, D
Can associate words with their initial letter sounds
assessment_indicators: Completes letter copying exercises accurately
Participates in alphabet songs

topics.name: Chapter Introduction: Ammu and Her Friends
topics.summary: Introduces the chapter theme of Ammu and her friends at school through a coloring activity, setting the context for the unit.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Chapter Introduction: Ammu and Her Friends: Students are introduced to the chapter's theme by coloring a picture of Ammu and her friends at school.
mastery_signals: Can color within lines
Can identify key figures in the picture
assessment_indicators: Completes the coloring activity neatly

topics.name: Observing School Activities
topics.summary: Students observe and describe various activities children are engaged in at school, such as dancing and studying, enhancing observational and comprehension skills.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Observing School Activities: Students identify and describe various activities performed by children in a school setting, enhancing observational skills.
mastery_signals: Can accurately describe actions in the picture
Can answer questions about the scene
assessment_indicators: Identifies multiple activities shown in the illustrations

topics.name: Teacher's Arrival and Hiding Bittu
topics.summary: Describes the scenario where Ammu hides her pet squirrel, Bittu, as the teacher enters the classroom, and the teacher's instruction not to bring pets, teaching about classroom rules.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Teacher's Arrival and Hiding Bittu: Students learn about classroom rules and consequences through a story where Ammu hides her pet squirrel from the teacher.
common_misconceptions: Students might not understand why pets are not allowed in school.
mastery_signals: Can retell the story sequence
Can explain the teacher's instruction
assessment_indicators: Answers comprehension questions about the story's events and characters' actions

topics.name: Washing Off Colors
topics.summary: The teacher instructs the children to wash off the colors that Bittu spread, emphasizing cleanliness and following instructions after a playful incident.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Washing Off Colors: Students learn about cleanliness and following instructions as the teacher directs them to wash off colors after a playful incident.
mastery_signals: Can identify the problem and solution in the story
Understands the importance of washing
assessment_indicators: Explains why the children need to wash the colors

topics.name: Identifying Favorite Colors
topics.summary: Children express their favorite colors and identify colors on their bodies, linking personal preference with color recognition and basic self-description.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Identifying Favorite Colors: Students practice identifying and expressing their favorite colors, connecting color names to objects and personal preferences.
mastery_signals: Can name several colors
Can state their favorite color
assessment_indicators: Correctly identifies colors on their body or objects

topics.name: Coloring and Naming Colors
topics.summary: Students are asked to name and color pictures of clothes, and identify various colors in a mind-map format, reinforcing color vocabulary and visual association.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Coloring and Naming Colors: Students practice color recognition and naming by coloring clothes and identifying different colors in a visual exercise.
mastery_signals: Can name common colors
Can color objects appropriately
assessment_indicators: Accurately names and colors the clothing items
Lists multiple colors

topics.name: Washing at the Water Tap
topics.summary: Children wash their hands, legs, and face at a water tap, identifying body parts and colors associated with them, promoting hygiene awareness and vocabulary.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Washing at the Water Tap: Students observe and describe children washing at a tap, identifying body parts and colors, promoting hygiene awareness.
mastery_signals: Can identify body parts being washed
Understands the purpose of washing
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the children's actions and dialogue

topics.name: Washing Hands and Face Song
topics.summary: Students sing a song about washing hands and face, reinforcing hygiene practices through a musical and action-based activity.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Washing Hands and Face Song: Students learn and sing a song about personal hygiene, specifically washing hands and face, with accompanying actions.
mastery_signals: Can sing the song with actions
Can identify who is washing
assessment_indicators: Participates in singing and performing actions

topics.name: Extending the Washing Song
topics.summary: Students extend a song about washing by adding lines for different body parts, practicing creative writing and reinforcing hygiene vocabulary.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Extending the Washing Song: Students creatively add new lines to a song about washing, focusing on different body parts and promoting language development.
common_misconceptions: Students might struggle with rhyming or fitting new words into the song structure.
mastery_signals: Can generate new lines for the song
Uses appropriate vocabulary for body parts
assessment_indicators: Creates new verses that fit the song's theme and rhythm

topics.name: Identifying Body Parts
topics.summary: Students identify and write the names of various body parts, promoting anatomical vocabulary and understanding of personal hygiene.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Identifying Body Parts: Students learn and write the names of different body parts, reinforcing vocabulary and understanding the importance of keeping the body clean.
common_misconceptions: Students may misspell body part names.
mastery_signals: Can correctly label body parts
Can pronounce body part names
assessment_indicators: Accurately writes the names of body parts on a diagram

topics.name: Apologizing for Bringing a Pet
topics.summary: Ammu apologizes to the teacher for bringing her pet to class, demonstrating understanding of rules and expressing regret, teaching social etiquette.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Apologizing for Bringing a Pet: Students learn about classroom rules and the importance of apologizing when rules are broken, through Ammu's interaction with her teacher.
common_misconceptions: Students might not understand the concept of apology or why it's necessary.
mastery_signals: Can explain why Ammu apologized
Understands the teacher's instruction
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the dialogue and the lesson learned

topics.name: Drawing and Talking About Pets
topics.summary: Students are encouraged to draw their pets and discuss them, fostering personal expression, communication skills, and connection to the story's theme.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Drawing and Talking About Pets: Students engage in creative expression by drawing their pets and developing oral communication skills by talking about them.
mastery_signals: Can draw a recognizable pet
Can describe their pet using simple sentences
assessment_indicators: Presents a drawing and shares a few sentences about their pet

topics.name: Activities at School
topics.summary: Introduces various activities children do at school, such as reading, writing, talking, and playing, reinforcing vocabulary and understanding of school life.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Activities at School: Students learn about and identify common activities performed at school, including reading, writing, talking, and playing, expanding their vocabulary.
mastery_signals: Can name different school activities
Can identify activities from pictures
assessment_indicators: Matches activity words to corresponding images

topics.name: Extending School Activities Song
topics.summary: Students add lines to a song about school activities, including eating, drinking, singing, and dancing, promoting creative expression and vocabulary related to daily routines.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Extending School Activities Song: Students creatively add new lines to a song about various school activities like eating, drinking, singing, and dancing, enhancing language skills.
common_misconceptions: Students might struggle with rhyming or maintaining the song's rhythm.
mastery_signals: Can generate new lines for the song about school activities
Uses action verbs correctly
assessment_indicators: Creates new verses that fit the song's theme and structure

topics.name: Categorizing School Activities
topics.summary: Students choose from a list of activities and write them in boxes corresponding to pictures of children performing those actions at school, reinforcing vocabulary and observational skills.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Categorizing School Activities: Students identify and match various school activities from a given list to corresponding images, reinforcing vocabulary and observational skills.
common_misconceptions: Students might misinterpret the actions in the pictures.
mastery_signals: Can correctly match activities to images
Can read and understand activity words
assessment_indicators: Accurately fills in the boxes with the correct activity words

topics.name: Letter Recognition Song (M, N, O, P)
topics.summary: Students sing a song that helps them identify letters M, N, O, P and associate them with corresponding words like 'Mat', 'monkey', 'Net', 'nest', 'Ox', 'owl', 'Pen', and 'pencil', enhancing phonological awareness and vocabulary.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Letter Recognition Song (M, N, O, P): Students learn to identify and associate letters M, N, O, P with specific words through a song, enhancing phonological awareness and vocabulary.
common_misconceptions: Students might confuse similar-looking letters or sounds.
mastery_signals: Can identify letters M, N, O, P
Can name words starting with these letters
assessment_indicators: Sings the song and correctly points to the letters/words

topics.name: Reading and Copying Letters (M, N, O, P)
topics.summary: Students practice reading and copying uppercase and lowercase letters M, N, O, P, along with associated words and pictures, developing early literacy and fine motor skills.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 English: Ammu and Her Friends at Her School — Reading and Copying Letters (M, N, O, P): Students practice letter recognition and handwriting by reading and copying uppercase and lowercase letters M, N, O, P and related vocabulary.
common_misconceptions: Students might struggle with correct letter formation or spacing.
mastery_signals: Can correctly write uppercase and lowercase letters
Can read the associated words
assessment_indicators: Accurately copies letters and words into the provided spaces

topics.name: Concluding the Zoo Visit
topics.summary: The story concludes with Ammu's family leaving the zoo and saying goodbye to Tinku, reinforcing narrative sequence.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: AT THE ZOO — Concluding the Zoo Visit: Students follow the narrative sequence of a story as Ammu's family departs from the zoo, practicing comprehension skills.
mastery_signals: Recalls the ending of the story
Understands the concept of farewell
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the story's conclusion

topics.name: Alphabet Recognition and Handwriting (U-Z)
topics.summary: Students engage in a song and tracing activity to learn and practice the letters U, V, W, X, Y, Z and associated vocabulary.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: AT THE ZOO — Alphabet Recognition and Handwriting (U-Z): Students learn letter identification, phonics, and handwriting for the final letters of the alphabet through songs and tracing exercises.
common_misconceptions: Students may confuse similar-looking letters like V and W
mastery_signals: Correctly identifies and writes letters
Associates letters with corresponding words
assessment_indicators: Traces letters accurately
Copies words legibly

topics.name: Alphabetical Order and Fine Motor Skills (Dot-to-Dot)
topics.summary: Students connect dots in alphabetical order from 'a' to 'z' to reveal an animal, then color it and write its name.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: AT THE ZOO — Alphabetical Order and Fine Motor Skills (Dot-to-Dot): Students practice sequencing the alphabet and develop fine motor skills by connecting dots to form an animal.
common_misconceptions: Students may skip letters or connect dots out of order
mastery_signals: Connects dots in correct alphabetical sequence
Identifies the formed animal
assessment_indicators: Completes the dot-to-dot puzzle accurately

topics.name: Identifying Fruits and Vegetables through Coloring
topics.summary: Students color outlines of common fruits and vegetables, reinforcing visual recognition.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Identifying Fruits and Vegetables through Coloring: Students practice visual recognition and fine motor skills by coloring pictures of various fruits and vegetables.
mastery_signals: Colors within lines
Identifies the items correctly
assessment_indicators: Completes coloring tasks accurately

topics.name: Visiting a Market
topics.summary: A narrative about Ammu visiting a market, observing various fruits and vegetables, and answering questions about the scene.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Visiting a Market: Students comprehend a story about a market visit, identifying different fruits and vegetables and answering related questions.
mastery_signals: Recalls details from the market scene
Identifies various produce
assessment_indicators: Answers comprehension questions about the market visit

topics.name: Expressing Food Preferences
topics.summary: A dialogue where Ammu expresses her dislike for bitter gourd curry, exploring personal tastes and family interactions.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Expressing Food Preferences: Students understand a dialogue about food preferences, learning to express likes and dislikes in a social context.
mastery_signals: Identifies Ammu's food preference
Explains why Ammu is not eating
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the dialogue and characters' feelings

topics.name: Importance of Healthy Eating
topics.summary: Ammu's grandmother explains the health benefits of eating fruits and vegetables, encouraging healthy habits.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Importance of Healthy Eating: Students learn about the benefits of consuming fruits and vegetables through a dialogue with Ammu's grandmother, promoting healthy lifestyle choices.
common_misconceptions: Students may not understand the long-term benefits of healthy eating
mastery_signals: Explains why fruits and vegetables are good for health
Recalls grandmother's advice
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the importance of healthy food

topics.name: Describing Fruits through Song
topics.summary: A song about apples and grapes, describing their appearance, taste, and shape, enhancing fruit identification and descriptive vocabulary.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Describing Fruits through Song: Students learn descriptive vocabulary and fruit characteristics by singing a song about apples and grapes.
mastery_signals: Sings the song correctly
Identifies apples and grapes
assessment_indicators: Recalls descriptive words for fruits

topics.name: Categorizing Fruits and Vegetables
topics.summary: Students sort a list of food items into categories of 'fruits' and 'vegetables', developing classification skills.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Categorizing Fruits and Vegetables: Students develop logical reasoning and classification skills by sorting various food items into 'fruits' and 'vegetables'.
common_misconceptions: Students may confuse fruits that are commonly used as vegetables (e.g., tomato, brinjal)
mastery_signals: Correctly categorizes all items
Explains reasoning for categorization
assessment_indicators: Accurately places items under 'fruits' or 'vegetables' headings

topics.name: Story: Princess Tomato's Award
topics.summary: A story about different vegetables (bitter gourd, lady's finger, red chilli, potato) presenting their qualities to win an award, teaching about attributes and self-presentation.
topics.embedding_text: Grade 1 General Knowledge: FRUITS AND VEGETABLES — Story: Princess Tomato's Award: Students engage with a narrative where vegetables describe their unique qualities, fostering comprehension and understanding of descriptive attributes.
mastery_signals: Recalls the claims of each vegetable
Identifies the main characters
assessment_indicators: Answers questions about the story's plot and characters

subtopics.name: Coloring Ammu and her family
subtopics.summary: An activity where students color a picture of Ammu and her family.

subtopics.name: Ammu's daily life and family interactions
subtopics.summary: A visual narrative depicting Ammu's interactions with her family members in various settings.

subtopics.name: Doggy chasing Bittu
subtopics.summary: A scene depicting Ammu's dog chasing the squirrel, Bittu.

subtopics.name: Bittu and Ammu introduce themselves
subtopics.summary: Dialogue between Ammu and Bittu where they introduce their names.

subtopics.name: Bittu's stay with Ammu
subtopics.summary: Dialogue about Bittu staying with Ammu and identifying family members.

subtopics.name: Singing about family roles
subtopics.summary: A song that helps students learn about different family members and their roles.

subtopics.name: Learning specific names of Ammu's family
subtopics.summary: Introduces the specific names of Ammu's grandfather, grandmother, father, mother, and brother.

subtopics.name: Matching names to characters
subtopics.summary: Students identify and read the names of Ammu, Bittu, Chintu, and Doggy.

subtopics.name: Drawing and writing one's own name
subtopics.summary: An activity for students to draw their own picture and write their name.

subtopics.name: Singing alphabet songs with associated words
subtopics.summary: Songs introducing letters A, B, C, D and words that start with them (e.g., Apple, Bat, Cat, Dog).

subtopics.name: Reading and copying letters and words
subtopics.summary: Practice in reading and writing uppercase and lowercase letters A, B, C, D, along with associated words.

subtopics.name: General alphabet song
subtopics.summary: A song to reinforce the entire alphabet sequence.

subtopics.name: Singing the Alphabet Song (U-Z)
subtopics.summary: A song to help students remember the letters U, V, W, X, Y, Z and their sounds.

subtopics.name: Tracing and Copying Letters and Words
subtopics.summary: Practice tracing and copying uppercase and lowercase letters U-Z, along with associated vocabulary words.

subtopics.name: Ammu asks for a story
subtopics.summary: Ammu asks her grandmother for a story about fruits and vegetables.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Princess Tomato's Award
subtopics.summary: The story begins with Mr. Pumpkin and Princess Tomato hosting an award for the most well-behaved vegetable.

subtopics.name: Bitter Gourd's Claim
subtopics.summary: Bitter gourd presents itself, highlighting its health benefits.

subtopics.name: Lady's Finger's Claim
subtopics.summary: Lady's finger presents itself, claiming to make people clever.

subtopics.name: Red Chilli's Claim
subtopics.summary: Red chilli presents itself, emphasizing its ability to add taste to curries.

subtopics.name: Potato's Claim
subtopics.summary: Potato presents itself, asserting its popularity.

exercises.text: This exercise tests comprehension of the introductory story by asking students to identify characters and actions in the provided picture.
This exercise assesses comprehension of the narrative about the squirrel and dog by asking questions about the events and characters.
This exercise evaluates understanding of the dialogue between Ammu and Bittu by asking about their conversation.
This exercise tests comprehension of the dialogue and social interaction by asking students to infer responses in a conversation between Ammu and Bittu.
This exercise assesses comprehension of the family song by asking students to identify the people mentioned and the subject of the song.
This exercise prompts students to recall and state the names of Ammu's family members and reflect on their own family members' names.
This activity requires students to identify and list their own family members from a given selection, applying classification skills.
This activity asks students to tick the family members who live in their home and then count the total number of family members, combining identification and basic counting skills.
This exercise provides practice in reading and copying the uppercase and lowercase forms of letters A, B, C, D, along with associated words.
Colour the picture.
1. What are the children doing? 2. What are the things you do at school?
1. What is Ammu doing? 2. What is she saying to Bittu? 3. What would the teacher say?
1. What do you see in the picture? 2. What is the teacher saying? 3. Will the children say anything? 4. What would they do now?
1. Where are the children going? 2. What are they saying? 3. Which colour do you like?
What are these? Name them. Colour the pictures and name the colours. What colours can you name?
1. Where are the children now? 2. What are they doing? 3. What is Fatima saying to Ganga? 4. What is Ganga saying to Fatima?
1) Who is washing the hands? 2) Who is washing the face?
Add lines to the song and sing. What is Hari singing? Wash... This is the way ...ear What is Ganga singing?
Write the names of the body parts. nose mouth hand leg eye neck head fingers knee shoulder
1) What is the teacher saying to Ammu? 2) What is Ammu saying?
Draw your pet and talk about it.
Add lines and sing the song. We are at school, ... eating ... drinking ... singing ... dancing
What are these children doing? Choose from the list and write in the boxes. eating drinking reading skipping playing dancing
Read and copy (letters M, N, O, P and associated words like Mango, monkey, Net, nest, Ox, owl, Pen, pencil).
Identify birds based on descriptions like 'It has green feathers. It has a red beak.' (parrot) and 'It has a blue neck. It has colourful feathers.' (peacock).
Look at the birds (crow, sparrow, crane, duck) and talk about their features like black feathers, sharp beak, grey feathers, small beak, white feathers, long legs, flat beak.
Answer questions about what is seen in the picture, who Ammu's new friend is, and if Bittu will go with Tinku.
Answer questions about what food they eat, what Tinku likes to eat, and what food you like.
Answer questions about where Ammu's family is now, what Bittu and Ammu are saying to Tinku, and what Tinku is saying to Bittu and Ammu.
Sing a song about the letters U, V, W, X, Y, Z and words associated with them (umbrella, van, watch, X-mas, yak, zoo).
Read and copy the letters U, V, W, X, Y, Z and associated words (umbrella, uniform, van, vase, watch, well, X-mas tree, X-ray, yak, yellow, zebra, zip).
Join the dots from 'a' to 'z' to get an animal, color it, and write its name in the given box.
Colour the pictures of an eggplant, pumpkin, pear, peach, bananas, and apple.
Answer questions about where Ammu is now, if you have ever been to a market, what fruits and vegetables are seen in the picture, and if you grow any of these at home.
Answer questions about what happened to Ammu, why she is not eating, and if it is right for Ammu to leave the table.
Answer questions about what grandmother is doing, what she told Ammu, and why it is good to eat all fruits and vegetables.
Sing Bittu's song about apples (big, round, red, sweet) and grapes (round, small, green, sour).

Read the words (pomegranate, watermelon, tomato, bitter gourd, cabbage, carrot, grapes, banana, brinjal, apple, mango, lady's fingers, custard apple, potato, pumpkin) and write them under the headings 'Names of the fruits' and 'Names of the vegetables'.
Answer questions about what Ammu asks her grandmother and what kind of stories you like.
